SEARCH
Package shot_boxes.informations

Class Informations

java.lang.Object
shot_boxes.informations.Informations
All Implemented Interfaces:
ShotBoxes
Direct Known Subclasses:
EasyInformations, HardInformations, MidInformations
public abstract class Informations extends Object implements ShotBoxes
The Informations abstract class represents info boxes. These objects fall from the top of the screen and can be collected by the player for points.

Subclasses determine the difficulty (Easy, Medium, Hard).

Version:
1.0
Author:

      public boolean isMoving

  • Constructor Details

    • Informations

      public Informations()

  • Method Details

    • uploudInformations

      public static void uploudInformations(String filePath)
      Loads informational content from a text file. The file should be structured with difficulty headers ("Easy", "Medium", "Hard") followed by lines of text information.
      Parameters:
      filePath - the path to the information file

    • draw

      public void draw(Image image, Graphics g)

    • interact

      public void interact(KnowledgeKeepers k)
      Handles interaction with the information box.

      Stops its movement, resets the vertical position (Y), and updates the KnowledgeKeeper to indicate no active info object remains.

      Specified by:
      interact in interface ShotBoxes
      Parameters:
      k - the KnowledgeKeeper who shooted this ShotBox

    • getEasyInformations

      public static List<Informations> getEasyInformations()
      Returns the list of easy informations.
      Returns:
      a list of easy information objects

    • getMidInformations

      public static List<Informations> getMidInformations()
      Returns the list of medium informations.
      Returns:
      a list of medium information objects

    • getHardInformations

      public static List<Informations> getHardInformations()
      Returns the list of hard informations.
      Returns:
      a list of hard information objects

    • getCoordinate

      public int[] getCoordinate()
      Returns the current coordinates of the item.
      Specified by:
      getCoordinate in interface ShotBoxes
      Returns:
      an array with two elements: X and Y positions

    • getInfo

      public String getInfo()
      Returns the information text.
      Returns:
      the info string

    • getPoint

      public int getPoint()
      Returns the point value of the information item.
